title: Heavy fines for Bafana after W/Cup DC transgressions
original_text:
Bafana’s losses will stretch beyond dropping all three points from their 2026 FIFA World Cup opening defeat with the tournament Disciplinary Code being imposed to severe effects.

Hugo Broos’ tactics turned sour as Bafana succumbed to a 2-0 defeat to Mexico.

While the result was expected across the world – except for some in South Africa – the three red cards issued by Brazilian referee Wilton Sampaio were not.

Two of them were for Bafana players Sphephelo Sithole and Themba Zwane while Teboho Mokoena walked away with a yellow.

Both Sithole and Zwane will now miss the game against Czech Republic on June 18 in Atlanta and the latter might face further sanctions.

Beyond that and according to the FIFA Disciplinary Code there will be fines of USD20 000 (approx. R326 000) each for Sithole and Zwane after both were handed straight red cards.

Mokoena will be fined USD10 000 (approx. R163 000) for his yellow card.

"For the sake of good order, it is to be noted that the list of disciplinary measures developed in the present annexe is not exhaustive, nor binding, and is without prejudice to the general principles established under article 25 of this Code.

"As a matter of fact, the decisions are rendered on a case-by-case basis and the type and extent of the disciplinary measures are determined by the relevant judicial body in accordance with the objective and subjective elements of the offence, taking into account both aggravating and mitigating circumstances,” further reads the Code.

"Disciplinary infringements are dealt with in compliance with the FIFA Disciplinary Code in force as well as with all relevant circulars and directives, with which the Participating Member Associations and Delegation Members undertake to comply," states the regulations.
_note: 全文走 summary 块A(LLM三判后只增不改);processed=成熟仓库累积,raw=本次收集过程。
